title: 7 Top-Rated Herbal Tinctures to Combat Dry Cough Naturally

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated airways, reducing coughing and relieving discomfort.
Flavonoids are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated throat, reducing the discomfort and pain associated with dry cough.

Polyphenols are good for dry cough because they have powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the throat, reduce swelling, and calm the cough reflex, making it easier to breathe and recover from dry cough symptoms.
Saponins are good for dry cough because they help loosen and break down mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and irritation in the throat and lungs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture, also known as licorice root tincture, is often used to help soothe a dry cough. The main active ingredient in this tincture, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the throat and airways. This can make it easier to breathe and help calm a dry, irritated cough. Glycyrrhizin also has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in the throat, which can help to calm a cough and reduce discomfort. Additionally, licorice root has been traditionally used to help treat respiratory issues, including coughs and bronchitis, due to its ability to thin mucus and make it easier to expel. When taken in a tincture form, the glycyrrhizin in licorice root can quickly absorb into the bloodstream, providing fast relief from a dry cough.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tincture that helps with dry cough, start by gathering dried Sambucus nigra flowers, roots, or berries, which are rich in antiviral properties and expectorants that can help loosen mucus and ease coughing. Begin by measuring one part of dried Sambucus nigra and three parts of a neutral spirit, such as vodka or rum, into a clean glass jar. Make sure the jar is airtight to prevent contamination. Next, store the mixture in a cool, dark place, like a cupboard or a shelf, and let it steep for two to three weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This will allow the Sambucus nigra to extract its medicinal properties into the spirit. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, dilute the tincture with water to the desired strength and use it as needed to help alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for dry cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Alkylamides are good for dry cough because they help soothe and calm the throat, reducing inflammation and promoting mucus production to relieve congestion and coughing.

Quercetin is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort of a dry cough.
Cichoric acid is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving the persistent cough.
Kaempferol is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the throat and ease the dry, itchy sensation that often accompanies a dry cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is a natural remedy that can help with a dry cough. This plant has been used for centuries to boost the immune system and fight off infections. When you have a dry cough, it's often because your body is trying to get rid of something that's irritating your throat and airways. Echinacea purpurea tincture can help by reducing inflammation and soothing the throat. The active compounds in the plant, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that can calm down the irritated tissues in your throat, making it easier to swallow and breathe. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tincture can also help to reduce mucus production, which can be beneficial when you have a dry cough. By taking Echinacea purpurea tincture, you may experience relief from your dry cough and other symptoms associated with it.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tincture, you will need dried Elderberry flowers or berries and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, measure out one part of dried Sambucus nigra flowers and add it to two parts of the solvent in a clean glass jar, making sure the flowers are completely covered. Next, seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for about two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the solvent draw out the plant's medicinal properties. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. To make a usable tincture, you can dilute the liquid with more solvent, if needed, and transfer it to a smaller bottle for storage. The resulting Sambucus nigra tincture can be taken to help alleviate dry cough symptoms, typically by mixing a few drops of the tincture with water or another liquid and swallowing it.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for dry cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Thymol is good for dry cough because it has antitussive properties that help to reduce inflammation and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, relieving the discomfort associated with dry cough.
Carvacrol is good for dry cough because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and calm the irritated throat and airways.

Rosmarinic acid is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the irritated throat, making it easier to breathe and alleviate the discomfort of a dry cough.
Tannins are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of a dry cough.
Luteolin is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the throat and reduce swelling, making it easier to cough up mucus and relieve dry, irritated airways.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris, also known as thyme, is a natural remedy that can help soothe a dry cough. The tincture of thyme is made by soaking the leaves and flowers of the thyme plant in a liquid, usually ethanol or glycerin. This liquid extract contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken internally, thymus vulgaris tincture can help to calm the cough and reduce inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe. The antibacterial properties of thyme can also help to fight off any underlying infections that may be causing the cough. Additionally, thyme has expectorant properties, which means it can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up. This can be especially helpful for people with dry coughs caused by respiratory infections, allergies, or colds.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Sambucus nigra herbal tincture to help with a dry cough, start by gathering the dried flowers of the Sambucus nigra plant, also known as elderflower. Use one part of the dried flowers to two parts of a solvent like vodka, brandy, or glycerin in a clean glass jar. Add the dried flowers to the jar and seal it tightly. Let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the solvent extract the medicinal properties from the flowers. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken in small amounts, typically 10-20 drops, three to four times a day, mixed with water or taken directly under the tongue to help soothe a dry cough. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or take medications.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal tinctures for dry cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for dry cough because it has natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to cough up irritants.
Quercetin is good for dry cough because it has powerful anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated throat, reducing coughing and making it easier to breathe.

Polyphenols are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce throat irritation and soothe the airways, allowing for easier coughing and relief from dry cough symptoms.

Tannins are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the irritated tissues in the throat, reducing the discomfort and inflammation associated with a dry cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is a natural remedy that can help with a dry cough. The eucalyptus plant contains a chemical called eucalyptol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the throat. When taken as a tincture, eucalyptol is absorbed into the body and can help to thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up. This can be especially helpful for people with a dry cough, as it can help to loosen up any mucus that may be present in the throat. Additionally, eucalyptol has a decongestant effect, which can help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to breathe. By reducing inflammation and thinning out mucus, eucalyptus globulus tincture can provide quick relief from a dry cough. It is often used in combination with other natural remedies to help alleviate symptoms of a cold or flu.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Sambucus nigra tincture that helps with dry cough, you will need some fresh or dried elderflower, a clean glass jar, a solvent such as vodka or glycerin, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid for storing the finished tincture. First, you need to prepare the elderflower, either by picking some fresh flowers or using dried elderflower in a powdered or whole form. If using fresh flowers, make sure to clean them well and remove any stems or leaves. Then, place the elderflower in the glass jar and pour enough solvent over it to cover the flowers completely. The general ratio for making a tincture is 1 part plant material to 2-3 parts solvent. Let the mixture sit for 2-6 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every day or two. After this time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Sambucus nigra tincture is now ready to use, and you can take it as needed to help soothe a dry cough.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal tinctures for dry cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the mucous membranes in the throat and reduce coughing.
Flavonoids are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling in the throat and ease congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieve the dry cough.

Tannins are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort of a dry cough.
Iridoids are good for dry cough because they help soothe and calm irritated throats, reducing inflammation and congestion, making it easier to breathe and recover from the cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tincture, also known as mullein, is a natural remedy that can help with dry cough. The plant's leaves and flowers contain saponins, which are substances that have anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink mullein tincture, the saponins travel to your lungs and help to reduce inflammation and irritation that can cause dry cough. This can help to loosen and clear mucus from your airways, making it easier to breathe. Mullein tincture also has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in your throat, which can help to calm a dry cough and reduce discomfort. Additionally, mullein's expectorant properties help to thin and clear mucus from your lungs, making it easier to cough up. By reducing inflammation and loosening mucus, mullein tincture can provide quick relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tincture that helps with dry cough, start by gathering fresh or dried elder flowers (Sambucus nigra). If you're using fresh flowers, dry them first by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dark place. Once your flowers are dry, add them to a clean glass jar with a 1:3 to 1:5 ratio of flowers to a neutral solvent like vodka or glycerin. For example, use 1 part dried flowers and 3 or 5 parts vodka. Make sure to label the jar with the date and ingredients. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the solvent extract the active compounds from the flowers. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This is your Sambucus nigra tincture, which can be taken 2-3 times a day to help soothe a dry cough.
remedies.image_alt: Verbascum thapsus herbal tinctures for dry cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for dry cough because it helps to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from scratchy, irritated throats and coughs.
Roscmarinic acid is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated throat, reducing the severity and frequency of coughing.

Menthone is good for dry cough because it has natural decongestant and expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the throat and airways, providing relief from dry, irritating coughs.
Polyphenols are good for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving the cough.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tincture, also known as peppermint oil, is often used to relieve dry cough. The reason for this is that peppermint oil has natural soothing properties that can calm and relax the throat muscles. When you have a dry cough, the muscles in your throat can get irritated, making it harder to cough up mucus. The menthol in peppermint oil helps to numb the throat and reduce inflammation, making it easier to breathe and cough. Additionally, peppermint oil has expectorant properties, which means it can help loosen and bring up mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and clear out. This can be especially helpful when you have a dry cough that's not producing much mucus. By using Mentha x piperita tincture, you may be able to find some relief from a dry cough and make it easier to breathe and feel more comfortable.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tincture, start by gathering fresh or dried elder flowers. If using fresh flowers, gently wash them in cold water and pat them dry with a paper towel. Next, place the flowers in a clean glass jar and cover them with a high-proof spirit like vodka or brandy. The general ratio is to use one part of flowers to four parts of spirit. Fill the jar completely, making sure the flowers are covered, then seal the jar tightly. Store the jar in a cool, dark place, shaking it gently every day for two to three weeks. This process is called maceration, and it helps to extract the active compounds from the flowers into the spirit. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. To use the tincture for a dry cough, take 20 to 30 drops in water or tea three to four times a day, as directed by a healthcare professional.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal tinctures for dry cough

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort caused by dry cough.

Tannins are good for dry cough because they have antitussive properties, which help to calm and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and relieving the irritation that causes dry coughing.
Polyphenols are good for dry cough because they have antitussive and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the irritated throat and airways, reducing the need to cough.

Isorhapontigenin is good for dry cough because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat, making it easier to cough up mucus and relieve dryness and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tincture is a natural remedy for dry cough. It comes from the elder tree, which has been used for centuries to treat respiratory problems. The tincture contains compounds like sambunigrin and sambutriol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When you take Sambucus nigra tincture, these compounds help to reduce inflammation in the airways and soothe the mucous membranes. This helps to thin out mucus and make it easier to cough up, providing quick relief from a dry, stubborn cough. The anti-inflammatory properties also help to calm the airways and make breathing easier, which can be especially helpful if you have a dry cough that's making it hard to sleep.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Sambucus nigra herbal tincture for dry cough relief, start by gathering high-quality dried elder flowers or berries. You will need a clean glass jar with a lid, a measuring cup, and a high-proof alcohol like vodka or glycerin. Add one part of dried Sambucus nigra to four parts of the alcohol in the jar, making sure the herbs are completely submerged. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the herbs release their active properties. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken in small doses, typically 20-30 drops, mixed with water or another liquid, several times a day to help soothe a dry cough and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal tinctures for dry cough

intro_desc: A dry cough can be a real nuisance, making it hard to sleep and even affecting your daily activities. Herbal tinctures can be a great way to help alleviate a dry cough. For instance, Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, is a natural anti-inflammatory that can soothe an irritated throat and reduce coughing. Echinacea purpurea, or purple coneflower, is another herb that can help boost your immune system, which can help your body fight off the underlying cause of the cough. When you're feeling under the weather, it's essential to address the issue promptly. A persistent cough can lead to fatigue, dehydration, and even complications like pneumonia. By using herbal tinctures like those containing Glycyrrhiza glabra and Echinacea purpurea, you can help calm your cough and get back to your normal routine. This can improve your sleep, energy levels, and overall quality of life. By taking proactive steps to manage your dry cough, you can reduce your risk of developing more serious health issues and get back to enjoying your daily activities.
supplementary_best: To remedy a dry cough, a combination of herbal tinctures can be effective. The best combination includes Eucalyptus globulus, Mentha x piperita, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. Eucalyptus globulus helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up. Mentha x piperita has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and calm the cough. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and demulcent properties that help to protect and soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing discomfort and inflammation. This combination works well together because it addresses the root causes of a dry cough: excess mucus and inflammation. By combining these herbs, you can help to clear the airways, reduce inflammation, and soothe the throat, providing relief from dry cough symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have a dry cough, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that can dry out your throat further or make your cough worse. One example is 'Aconitum napellus', also known as monk's hood or wolf's bane. This herb can dry out your mucus membranes and make your cough worse. Another example is 'Digitalis purpurea', also known as foxglove. While it's sometimes used to treat heart problems, it can also dry out your throat and make it harder to cough up mucus. 'Ipecacuanha' is another herb to avoid, as it can irritate your throat and make your cough worse. 'Sanguinaria canadensis', also known as bloodroot, can also dry out your mucus membranes and make your cough worse. 'Ammi visnaga', also known as khella, can irritate your throat and make your cough worse. And finally, 'Iris germanica', also known as German iris, can irritate your throat and make your cough worse. These herbs are best avoided if you have a dry cough.
supplementary_other_remedies: Honey is a natural remedy that can help soothe a dry cough. It has a thick, sticky texture that coats the throat, providing temporary relief from irritation. When you swallow, the honey helps to trap moisture in the throat, giving you a chance to calm down and relax. This is especially helpful at night, when a dry cough can keep you awake.  Slippery elm is another natural remedy that can help with a dry cough. It's a type of tree bark that contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that can help to soothe and calm the throat. When you swallow, the mucilage expands to form a protective barrier, reducing inflammation and irritation. This can help to reduce the urge to cough, giving you a break from the discomfort.  Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling in the throat, making it a useful natural remedy for a dry cough. When you make a warm tea by steeping fresh ginger in hot water, the heat helps to loosen mucus and reduce inflammation. This can help to calm down the cough, making it easier to breathe.  Elderberry syrup is another natural remedy that can help with a dry cough. It's made from the berries of the elder tree, which contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. When you drink elderberry syrup, the compounds help to reduce inflammation in the throat, making it easier to breathe and reducing the urge to cough.  Licorice root is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to soothe a dry cough. It contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat. When you drink licorice root tea, the compounds help to calm down the cough, making it easier to breathe and relax.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with a dry cough may also experience other health issues, including a sore throat, difficulty swallowing, and a tickly sensation in the throat. This is often due to inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract.  One common issue is bronchitis, an infection of the airways that can lead to a persistent cough and difficulty breathing. Medicinal herbs like thyme and eucalyptus can help soothe bronchitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria. Thyme contains compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which help combat infections, while eucalyptus oil has decongestant properties that can ease congestion and open up airways.  Another issue people with dry cough may experience is asthma, a condition that causes the airways to become inflamed and narrow, making it difficult to breathe. Herbs like turmeric and ginger can help alleviate asthma symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the airways. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties, while ginger has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways.  People with dry cough may also experience sinusitis, an infection of the sinuses that can cause headaches, facial pain, and difficulty breathing through the nose. Herbs like peppermint and echinacea can help alleviate sinusitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Peppermint oil has decongestant properties that can ease sinus congestion, while echinacea is a natural immune system booster that can help combat infections.  In addition, people with dry cough may experience acid reflux, a condition that causes stomach acid to flow up into the throat, causing discomfort and pain. Herbs like slippery elm and licorice root can help alleviate acid reflux symptoms by soothing the digestive tract and reducing inflammation. Slippery elm has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the digestive tract, while licorice root has natural anti-acid properties that can help neutralize stomach acid.  Lastly, people with dry cough may experience allergies, which can cause the airways to become inflamed and narrow, making it difficult to breathe. Herbs like nettle and quercetin can help alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation in the airways. Nettle has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe allergic reactions, while quercetin is a natural antihistamine that can help reduce the body's allergic response.
